documents.title: Property Sale Deed Registration
documents.description: Mandatory registration of property sale at Sub-Registrar's office. Without registration, property transfer has no legal validity under Registration Act 1908.

required_documents.category: Both buyer and seller must provide
options: PAN Card (mandatory for transactions above ₹10 lakh)
Aadhaar Card / Voter ID / Passport (identity proof)
Two passport-size photographs each
Two witnesses with identity proofs
Original Title Deed / Previous Sale Deed
Property Tax Receipts (last 3 years, up to date)
Encumbrance Certificate (from Sub-Registrar for last 13–30 years)
Khata Certificate and Khata Extract (from municipal body)
Approved Building Plan (for built property)
Completion Certificate / Occupancy Certificate (for new construction)
NOC from Society/Builder (for apartment)
Water/Electricity clearance certificate
Bank loan sanction letter (if buyer is taking loan)
Demand Draft / Pay Order for stamp duty and registration fee payment
Form 26QB (TDS on property above ₹50 lakh — buyer deducts 1% TDS)

process_steps.title: Verify property title and get Encumbrance Certificate
process_steps.detail: Before any purchase, get Encumbrance Certificate (EC) from Sub-Registrar office for last 13 years minimum (30 years recommended). EC shows all registered transactions — loans, mortgages, prior sales. A clean EC means the property has no legal dues.
process_steps.tip: In Karnataka: kaveri.karnataka.gov.in | Maharashtra: igrmaharashtra.gov.in | Tamil Nadu: tnreginet.gov.in | AP/Telangana: registration.ap.gov.in

process_steps.title: Calculate and pay Stamp Duty
process_steps.detail: Stamp duty varies by state: typically 4–8% of sale value or circle rate (whichever is higher). Pay online via state's stamp duty portal or at authorised banks. Get e-stamp paper or franking done.
process_steps.tip: Maharashtra stamp duty: 5% (men) / 4% (women) + 1% metro cess + 1% LBT in some areas. Calculate at igrmaharashtra.gov.in.

process_steps.title: Draft the Sale Deed
process_steps.detail: Hire a licensed advocate or document writer to draft the sale deed. Must include: full property description, sale consideration, payment terms, possession date, indemnity clause, and both parties' details.
process_steps.tip: Do not use informal templates — errors in sale deed are very difficult and expensive to correct.

process_steps.title: Book appointment at Sub-Registrar Office
process_steps.detail: Most states have online appointment booking. Both buyer and seller (or power of attorney holder) must be physically present with originals. Two witnesses also required.

process_steps.title: Registration at Sub-Registrar's office
process_steps.detail: Staff verifies documents, photographs buyer and seller, takes thumb impressions, collects registration fee (usually 0.5–1% of property value), and registers the deed. You receive certified copy with registration number.
process_steps.tip: Registration fee: 1% of property value in Maharashtra (max ₹30,000 for some categories).

process_steps.title: Mutation (Khata Transfer) after registration
process_steps.detail: After sale deed registration, apply for Khata transfer at local municipal body with: registered sale deed copy, property tax receipts, filled mutation application. This is separate from registration and essential for paying property tax as new owner.
process_steps.tip: Mutation does not create title — only sale deed registration does. But mutation is needed for tax records.

documents.fees: Stamp duty: 4–8% of property value (state-specific) | Registration: 0.5–1%

documents.title: Land Records / 7/12 Extract / ROR (Record of Rights)
documents.description: Official document showing land ownership, cultivation rights, and encumbrances. Called 7/12 (Satbara) in Maharashtra, RTC in Karnataka, Patta in Tamil Nadu/AP/Telangana.

required_documents.category: To obtain Land Record extract (no documents required, public record)
options: Survey number / Gat number of the land
Village name and taluka
Owner's name (for search purposes)

required_documents.category: To apply for Mutation (name change in land records after purchase/inheritance)
options: Registered Sale Deed (for purchase)
Succession Certificate / Legal Heir Certificate (for inheritance)
Death Certificate of previous owner (for inheritance)
Court order (if via court decree)
Identity proof of new owner

process_steps.title: Access land records online (free, instant)
process_steps.detail: Maharashtra (7/12 Satbara): bhulekh.mahabhumi.gov.in | Karnataka (RTC/Pahani): landrecords.karnataka.gov.in | Tamil Nadu (Patta): eservices.tn.gov.in | Andhra Pradesh: meeseva.gov.in | Telangana: dharani.telangana.gov.in | Rajasthan: apnakhata.raj.nic.in | UP: upbhulekh.gov.in
process_steps.tip: You need Survey/Khasra/Gat number to search. This is usually on old sale deeds or property tax receipts.

process_steps.title: Get certified copy for official use
process_steps.detail: Online downloads are accepted for most purposes but may need certification for court/bank use. For certified copy: visit Talathi (Maharashtra) or Village Accountant (Karnataka) or Revenue Inspector office with application + nominal fee.
process_steps.tip: Digitally signed online extracts from official portals are considered legally valid certified copies.

process_steps.title: Apply for Mutation after purchase or inheritance
process_steps.detail: File Form for Mutation at Talathi / Revenue Inspector office → Attach sale deed/succession documents → Notice is given to existing parties → If no objection in 30 days → Mutation order passed → Records updated.
process_steps.tip: Mutation should be done within 3 months of purchase. Delays can complicate future sales.

documents.fees: Free for online view | ₹15–50 for certified copy
documents.helpline: Contact your local Talathi / Revenue Inspector / Tehsildar

documents.title: Encumbrance Certificate (EC)
documents.description: Certificate showing all registered transactions on a property over a specified period — loans, mortgages, sales, gifts. Proves property is free from legal dues.

required_documents.category: Information needed to apply (no documents required for public search)
options: Property details: Survey number / Plot number
Village/Town, Taluka, District
Period for which EC is required (specify start and end year)
Owner's name

process_steps.title: Apply online (available in most states)
process_steps.detail: Maharashtra: igrmaharashtra.gov.in | Karnataka: kaveri.karnataka.gov.in | Tamil Nadu: tnreginet.gov.in | AP: registration.ap.gov.in | Telangana: registration.telangana.gov.in. Log in (or guest search) → Enter property details → Select period → Pay fee → Download EC.
process_steps.tip: For property purchase: get EC for minimum 13 years. For 30-year EC, visit Sub-Registrar office as older records may not be digitised.

process_steps.title: Offline application at Sub-Registrar office
process_steps.detail: Fill Form 22 (or state equivalent) → Submit with property details and fee → EC issued within 3–7 working days.

process_steps.title: Interpret the EC
process_steps.detail: Form 15 EC = property has registered transactions (shows all entries). Form 16 Nil EC = no transactions found — property is free from encumbrances. Always verify that the current owner's name appears in the chain.
process_steps.tip: A gap in the ownership chain in EC is a red flag — consult a property lawyer before purchasing.

documents.fees: ₹200–500 for online | ₹400–1000 for offline (varies by state and period)
